name: De Novo Protein Design Success Rate
description: Percentage of computationally designed novel proteins that fold and function as intended when experimentally tested. Tracks progress from Rosetta through deep learning methods (ProteinMPNN, RFdiffusion).

data_points.source: Baker lab / Rosetta — early de novo protein design: Top7 was a breakthrough but success rates ~1-2% for novel folds
Baker lab — Rosetta improvements for small proteins, ~5% of designed sequences folding as intended
Baker lab — systematic de novo design of protein folds; 5/43 designs crystallized successfully (~10%)
Baker lab — designed fluorescent proteins, enzymes; success rates improving with larger computational screening
Baker lab — hallucination and constrained design methods; trRosetta + Rosetta pipeline ~25% experimental success
Baker lab — ProteinMPNN + RFdiffusion: ~50% of designed binders showing activity experimentally
Baker lab — RFdiffusion all-atom: designed proteins with small molecule binding, ~65% experimental success for simple targets
Baker lab / DeepMind — continued improvements; ProteinMPNN + AlphaFold2 validation pipeline achieving ~75% for constrained design problems (approx)
